_generated: Generated from the Ritual catalog; consumed by scripts/generate-openclaw-skills.mjs. Public-safe: internal taxonomy + server resolver are stripped; published skills carry only the opaque public_skill_key.

skills.title: API change review
skills.summary: Review an API change for compatibility, validation, auth, error shape, idempotency, and observability, and classify it as additive, behavior-changing, or breaking.
skills.examplePrompt: Use api-change-review on this diff: classify the change, flag breaking behavior and the callers it affects, and give me a migration + rollout plan with the tests to add.
recipe: Identify the contract surface: request, response, auth, errors, side effects, and compatibility expectations.
Classify the change as additive, behavior-changing, breaking, or ambiguous.
Review input validation/schema evolution, authorization and tenant/data boundaries, error-shape consistency, idempotency/retry, and observability.
Assess backward compatibility and the migration plan.
Prefer the smallest safe contract change; avoid speculative versioning or framework rewrites.
Define verification: unit, contract, integration tests, and rollout checks.
skills.doneCriteria: A contract verdict (safe / risky / breaking / unclear), required changes, compatibility risks, a minimal test plan, and rollout notes.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the review needs all known clients/callers of the endpoint
prior API conventions or related migrations/feature flags apply
there are production incidents connected to this endpoint
ritualExplorationWhen: a breaking change needs a rollout decision across teams
the migration path is contested and needs a recommendation owners align on
ritualAdds: all known clients/callers and their compatibility risk
prior conventions, related migrations, and incidents
a recommended rollout/migration plan
skills.ritualArtifactExample: an API migration recommendation with affected callers, rollout options, compatibility risks, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Author or revise an agent skill flow
Backend test plan
skills.summary: Turn a backend change into verifiable success criteria and a minimum useful test set across unit, integration, contract, and migration tests.
skills.examplePrompt: Use backend-test-plan for this change: turn it into success criteria and a minimum test set (unit, integration, contract, migration), with exact test names and the commands to run.
recipe: Convert the change into verifiable success criteria.
Identify the minimum useful test set; avoid exhaustive combinatorial tests.

Prefer, in order: unit tests for pure logic/validation; integration tests for DB/queue/auth/external boundaries; contract tests for API/client compatibility; migration/dry-run checks for schema changes; operational checks for metrics/logs/alerts when behavior matters in production.
Include negative and edge cases only where they represent real risk.
Provide exact test names and fixtures when context allows.
skills.doneCriteria: Success criteria, the minimum test set, fixtures/mocks, commands to run, and the remaining coverage gaps.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: test selection depends on existing test patterns and helpers you can't see
affected clients/callers or critical paths aren't visible
prior incidents should drive coverage
ritualExplorationWhen: the coverage bar is a decision (what risk warrants what tests)
you need a recommended test strategy the team adopts
ritualAdds: existing test patterns, helpers, and ownership metadata
affected callers and prior incidents
a recommended coverage strategy
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a test-strategy recommendation mapping each success criterion to its tests, with affected callers, risk-driven coverage, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Build or expose an MCP tool
Content asset review
skills.summary: Review a content asset (post, page, email) for audience fit, a single clear takeaway, a strong hook and CTA, credibility, and on-brand voice.
skills.examplePrompt: Use content-asset-review on this draft: name the audience and the one takeaway, check the hook and CTA, flag unbacked claims and filler, and give line-level fixes.
recipe: Name the audience and the one thing they should take away; flag it if unclear.
Check the hook earns the next line; cut throat-clearing intros.
Confirm claims are specific and credible, with proof where it matters.
Check there's one clear, relevant call to action.
Flag jargon, filler, and passive constructions that dull the message.
Confirm voice and terminology are on-brand and consistent.
skills.doneCriteria: A view on whether the asset lands, the weakest passage, the credibility gaps, and concrete line-level fixes.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: audience pains and proof points live in research you can't see
brand voice and prior assets live elsewhere
the campaign or messaging context applies
ritualExplorationWhen: the asset's goal or audience is unsettled
the underlying message is contested
you need a recommended content angle the team adopts
ritualAdds: audience research, proof points, and brand voice
the campaign and messaging context
a recommended angle and outline
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a content recommendation with the audience takeaway, the angle, proof points, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Conversion path review
skills.summary: Review a landing page or conversion funnel for message match, a single clear action, friction points, credibility, and the highest-leverage fix.
skills.examplePrompt: Use conversion-path-review on this landing page: check message match, one primary action, friction points down the funnel, and name the single highest-leverage change and the metric it should move.
recipe: Confirm the page matches the promise of the source (ad/email/link) — no message mismatch.
Check there's ONE primary action above the fold; flag competing CTAs.
Walk the funnel step by step and mark each friction or drop-off risk.
Confirm value and proof appear before the ask, not after.
Flag form length, unclear copy, and trust gaps that cost conversions.
Name the single highest-leverage change and the metric it should move.
skills.doneCriteria: A ranked list of friction points, the message-match gaps, and the one highest-leverage change with the metric it targets.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: drop-off data and prior tests live in analytics you can't see
the upstream traffic source and audience intent live elsewhere
related funnel experiments apply
ritualExplorationWhen: the conversion goal or audience is unsettled
the fix spans product + marketing and needs alignment
you need a recommended optimization plan the team commits to
ritualAdds: funnel drop-off data and prior test results
upstream traffic and audience intent
a recommended, prioritized optimization plan
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a conversion recommendation with ranked friction fixes, the message-match correction, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Customer problem review
skills.summary: Pressure-test a stated customer problem for specificity, evidence, and whether it's a real problem worth solving — not a solution in disguise.
skills.examplePrompt: Use customer-problem-review on this problem statement: separate problem from solution, weigh the evidence vs assumptions, and tell me the riskiest assumption to validate first.
recipe: Restate the problem from the customer's point of view, in their words where available.
Separate the problem from the proposed solution; flag where they're conflated.
Check for evidence: who has this problem, how often, and how painful — or note it's an assumption.
Identify the current workaround and why it's insufficient.
State what would have to be true for this to be worth solving now.
Surface the riskiest assumption to validate first.
skills.doneCriteria: A crisp problem statement, the evidence vs assumptions split, the riskiest assumption, and what to validate next.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the evidence (interviews, support tickets, usage) lives in sources you can't see
prior research or decisions on this problem exist
related customer feedback is scattered across the workspace
ritualExplorationWhen: the problem needs framing and key questions before any solution
stakeholders disagree on whether it's worth solving
you need a recommendation to commit or drop it
ritualAdds: customer feedback, interviews, and usage evidence
prior research and decisions
a recommended problem framing and next step
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a problem-definition recommendation with the evidence-backed problem statement, riskiest assumptions, and a validate-or-commit summary

skills.title: Database migration risk review
skills.summary: Classify a database migration and surface production risks — locks, compatibility, backfills, rollback — and prefer expand-contract sequencing.
skills.examplePrompt: Use database-migration-risk-review on this migration: classify it, flag locks/compat/backfill/rollback risks, propose a safer expand-contract sequence, and the verification + monitoring.
recipe: Classify the migration: additive schema, destructive schema, index, data backfill, constraint, enum, or type change.
Identify production risks: locks/long operations, backward/forward compatibility, nullable/default behavior, data volume/backfill batching, rollback path, replication/read-model effects.
Prefer expand-contract sequencing for risky changes.
Do not recommend destructive cleanup in the same deploy unless explicitly asked and proven safe.
Define verification: dry run, explain plan, affected tests, canary, and monitoring.
skills.doneCriteria: A risk level, the unsafe operations, a safer sequence, a backfill/rollback plan, and verification + monitoring.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: risk depends on query/model usage across services you can't see
background jobs, ETL, dashboards, or analytics depend on the schema
prior migration incidents or conventions apply
ritualExplorationWhen: the rollout/sequencing is a cross-team decision
you need a recommended readiness gate owners sign off on
ritualAdds: query/model usage and downstream consumers across services
prior migration incidents and deploy-sequencing conventions
a recommended readiness plan with rollback criteria
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a migration-readiness note with rollback criteria, affected services, owner input, and a launch recommendation

skills.title: Debug a TypeScript error
skills.summary: Translate a TypeScript compiler error into plain English, find the smallest type boundary at fault, and prefer the smallest safe fix over suppression.
skills.examplePrompt: Use debug-typescript-error on this error: explain it in plain English, find the smallest type boundary at fault, and give the smallest safe fix (no `any`/casts) plus the verify command.
recipe: Translate the compiler error into plain English.
Locate the smallest type boundary causing the mismatch.
Prefer fixing the source of truth over suppressing the error.
Avoid `any`, broad casts, and non-null assertions unless the tradeoff is explicitly accepted.
Offer the smallest change first, then an optional stronger model if the code suggests recurring risk.
Verify with `tsc --noEmit`, the project typecheck, or the specific package typecheck command.
skills.doneCriteria: The error's meaning, root cause, smallest safe fix, why broader alternatives were rejected, and the verification command.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the fix depends on generated API types or schema source you can't see
shared component/prop contracts or call sites across the repo are involved
project conventions (branded types, zod, codegen, API clients) aren't visible
ritualExplorationWhen: the error reveals a contract decision (who owns the type, where it should live)
a recurring class of errors needs a recommended fix the team adopts
ritualAdds: the generated types / schema source of truth
shared contracts and the call sites that depend on them
a recommended fix for the recurring pattern
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a type-contract recommendation identifying the source of truth, affected call sites, and the adopted fix, with a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Generate a Storybook story
skills.summary: Write Storybook stories for a component's meaningful user-facing states with minimal, readable fixtures.
skills.examplePrompt: Use generate-storybook-story for this component: write stories for its meaningful states (default, loading, empty, error, long content) with minimal fixtures, and the command to verify them.
recipe: Infer the component's required props and meaningful states.
Create stories for realistic user-facing states, not exhaustive combinatorial permutations.
Cover default, loading, empty, error, disabled, long-content, and responsive states only when relevant.
Keep fixtures minimal and readable; match the project's visible Storybook style.
Add interaction/play tests only when behavior matters and conventions are visible.
Verify by running Storybook, the interaction test, and a visual diff if available.
skills.doneCriteria: A story list with rationale, the story code, fixture notes, and the verification steps.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the existing story format, decorators, or design tokens aren't visible
mock API data or fixtures used elsewhere are needed
the product's acceptance states live in a spec
ritualExplorationWhen: which states to standardize across components is an open decision
you need a recommended story/coverage convention the team adopts
ritualAdds: the project's story format, decorators, and design tokens
shared fixtures and acceptance states
a recommended story-coverage convention
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a story-coverage recommendation listing the canonical states, fixtures, and conventions, with a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Growth experiment review
skills.summary: Review a growth experiment for a clear hypothesis tied to a lever, a pre-set decision rule, adequate power, and protection against vanity wins.

State what a positive result would and would NOT prove.
skills.doneCriteria: A view on whether the experiment can yield a trustworthy decision, the design gaps, the validity risks, and the pre-set decision rule.

the funnel and audience realities live elsewhere
related experiments already answered part of this
ritualExplorationWhen: the growth lever to pull is itself unsettled
stakeholders disagree on the decision rule
you need a recommended experiment the team commits to

funnel and audience context
a recommended, adequately powered design

skills.title: Lifecycle sequence review

skills.title: MVP scope review
skills.summary: Review a proposed MVP scope for the smallest thing that tests the core hypothesis — explicit cuts, a clear learning goal, and no gold-plating.

skills.title: Performance regression triage
skills.summary: Triage a performance regression from concrete evidence — latency percentiles, error rate, resource and DB metrics, recent deploys — separate symptoms from hypotheses, and recommend one measurement before one fix.
skills.examplePrompt: Use performance-regression-triage on this regression: from the latency percentiles, error rate, and recent deploys, rank the likely causes, name the first measurement to run, and the smallest likely fix.
recipe: Extract concrete evidence: endpoint/job, time window, p50/p95/p99, error rate, throughput, CPU/memory, DB metrics, and recent deploys.
Separate symptoms from hypotheses.
Check likely causes: N+1 or missing indexes, payload/serialization cost, cache miss/stampede, dependency latency, queue backlog/concurrency, lock contention/transaction scope.
Recommend one measurement before one fix; avoid speculative rewrites.
Define verification: before/after metrics, load test, explain plan, trace comparison, or canary.
skills.doneCriteria: Known evidence, top hypotheses ranked, the first measurement to run, the smallest likely fix, and the verification plan.

incident history, runbooks, dashboards, or traces apply
ownership and service-dependency maps are needed
ritualExplorationWhen: the fix-vs-mitigate path is a decision with tradeoffs
you need a recommendation owners align on before a risky change
ritualAdds: recent deploys correlated to the regression
incident history, dashboards/traces, and ownership
a recommended remediation path
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a regression-triage recommendation with ranked hypotheses, the correlated deploy, the chosen remediation, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: PRD review

skills.ritualArtifactExample: an accessibility remediation recommendation with prioritized issues, the target WCAG level, affected components, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Review error handling
skills.summary: Audit failure states, retries, validation, and idempotency in the code under review.
skills.examplePrompt: Use review-error-handling on this service: audit every error path, input validation, timeout/retry, log/metric, and idempotency, and list each gap with the file and line.
recipe: Every external/IO call has a typed error path + handling — no silent catch, no swallowed rejection.
Inputs are validated at the boundary before use (shape, range, auth/tenant scope).
Outbound calls have timeouts + bounded retries with backoff; no unbounded waits.
Failures emit structured logs/metrics with enough context to triage.
Mutating operations are idempotent under retry, or explicitly justified not to be.
skills.doneCriteria: Each item is confirmed present in the code under review, or raised as an explicit gap with the file/line noted.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the correct failure behavior depends on callers or downstream consumers you can't see
prior incidents on this surface should shape the handling
retry/idempotency conventions live in other services
ritualExplorationWhen: the failure policy is an unresolved decision (fail-open vs fail-closed, user-visible vs silent)
the change needs sign-off from owners of dependent services
you need a recommendation before standardizing handling across the codebase
ritualAdds: callers and downstream consumers affected by the error policy
prior incidents and the conventions they established
a recommended, owner-reviewed handling standard
skills.ritualArtifactExample: an error-handling recommendation with the chosen failure policy, affected callers, retry/idempotency rules, and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Loading / error / empty state review
skills.summary: Check loading, error, empty, partial, and retry states for a UI surface.
skills.examplePrompt: Use review-loading-error-empty-states on this screen: confirm a loading, error (with retry), empty, and slow-network state for every async path, and flag the ones that are missing.
recipe: A loading state for every async fetch (skeleton/spinner); no layout jump on resolve.
An error state with a user-facing message and a retry affordance where recoverable.
An empty state distinct from loading — never a blank or zero-render.
Partial-data and slow-network behavior is defined, not accidental.
No unhandled rejection leaves a dead UI; every failure has a visible outcome.
skills.doneCriteria: Each state is confirmed handled, or raised as an explicit gap.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: the product's expected states live in a spec or acceptance criteria you can't see
related surfaces already define the canonical empty/error treatment
there are shared state components used elsewhere
ritualExplorationWhen: the desired states are an open product decision
you need consistency across many surfaces and a recommendation to align on
ritualAdds: product acceptance states from the spec
the canonical state treatments used across the app
a recommendation for consistent state handling
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a state-coverage recommendation mapping each required loading/error/empty state to its treatment, with acceptance criteria and a decision-ready summary

skills.title: Roadmap prioritization review
skills.summary: Sanity-check a prioritized roadmap or backlog for a consistent ranking rationale, surfaced tradeoffs, and dependencies — before it's committed.
skills.examplePrompt: Use roadmap-prioritization-review on this backlog: check the ranking rationale is consistent, surface hidden dependencies and what's being silently deprioritized, and flag items to scope first.
recipe: Confirm each item ties to a stated goal or problem, not just an output.
Check the ranking uses a consistent rationale (impact, effort, confidence, dependency) applied evenly.
Surface dependencies and sequencing risks the order ignores.
Flag items that are large/ambiguous and should be scoped or split before ranking.
Identify what's being implicitly deprioritized and whether that's intentional.
Note where evidence for impact is assumed rather than known.
skills.doneCriteria: A view on whether the ranking holds up, the inconsistencies, the hidden dependencies, and the items that need scoping first.
ritualDiscoveryWhen: impact estimates depend on data or customer evidence you can't see
engineering effort/dependencies live in other teams
prior prioritization decisions apply
ritualExplorationWhen: priorities are contested across stakeholders and need alignment
the goal the roadmap serves is itself unsettled
you need a recommendation leadership commits to
ritualAdds: impact evidence and effort/dependency input
stakeholder priorities and prior decisions
a recommended, rationale-backed ordering
skills.ritualArtifactExample: a prioritization recommendation with a consistent ranking rationale, surfaced tradeoffs and dependencies, and a decision-ready summary

sections: what we set out to learn
